title: Tinker missed a 200-item backlog accumulation for weeks: add queue-level trend metrics to Mode 1 pipeline health audit
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-NNN). Operator reviews PROP before implementation. Output: a focused PROP that adds queue-level trend metrics to tinker's Mode 1 audit, with concrete thresholds and findings-generation rules. Not the kitchen sink — this DIRECTIVE is narrow.
context: On 2026-05-10 the operator surfaced (via a casual read of the decider's daily-report mentioning 'several hundred things still in queue') that open-issues.json had grown to 230+ items, with ~120 of them in the 7-21d age band that PROP-026 was designed to drain. The conversation that followed produced PROP-026 (M1/M2/M3 backlog clearance), PROP-027 (5-action routing matrix), and HNOTE-driven drain of 61 orphans.

Tinker had run on 2026-05-07, 2026-05-09, and 2026-05-10 (operator-directive mode). All three Mode 1 quick-checks returned 'pipeline GREEN'. The 2026-05-09 report explicitly stated: 'GREEN. All scheduled agent outputs fresh: decider daily-report 78m ago, poller 3.5h, curmudgeon 3.9h, analyst 17h. Last 12 decider runs: ALL substantive. No-op rate ≈ 0%. Curmudgeon priority-queue empty.' Every individual metric tinker checked was healthy.

Tinker DOES have a Step 4: Audit Open Issues in monitor/prompts/reference/tinker-pipeline-health.md. It checks: Age (Major+ open > 7d), Repeated deferrals, Fixed-but-recurring, Issues without patches. None of these fired for the 230-item backlog because: (a) most items are minor/moderate (Major-only check missed minor accumulation), (b) items weren't 'deferred' — they were freshly-routed-to-analyst-then-sat, (c) they're not fixed-recurring — they're never-fixed, (d) they DO have patch hints in description, just no one wrote the patches.

Operator framing (2026-05-10T17:38Z): 'why didn't tinker notice that we had a 200 item queue that wasn't being burned down in the first place? that's one of its core functions.' Followed (T17:42Z) by: 'tinker never noticed. the system itself did.'

The legitimate critique: tinker's audit surface measured *liveness* (are agents running, are outputs fresh, no-op rates low) but never *throughput* (is the work-backlog growing or shrinking, week over week). A pipeline can be 100% live and still have a slowly-accumulating queue if inflow > outflow by a small amount over many cycles.
operator_intent: Add queue-level trend metrics to tinker's Mode 1 pipeline health audit. Threshold-triggered findings should fire BEFORE the backlog becomes operator-visible-by-accident. The audit is the entire goal of this DIRECTIVE — no other behavior changes proposed.

tasks.description: Specify the exact metrics tinker should compute each run and the data sources they come from.

proposed_metrics.open_issues_by_status: Group by .status field. {open, assigned-analyst, blocked-on-curmudgeon, partially-fixed-pending-verification, pending-human, monitoring}.
proposed_metrics.open_issues_age_distribution: Bucket by age: 0-2d, 2-7d, 7-14d, 14-30d, 30-90d, >90d. Compute from created_at OR found_at. Per-bucket count.
proposed_metrics.closed_issues_velocity_7d: Count of closed-issues.json entries with fixed_at within last 7d. Compute from closed-issues.json + closure-ledger.jsonl.
proposed_metrics.new_issues_velocity_7d: Count of open-issues.json entries with created_at OR found_at within last 7d.
proposed_metrics.assigned_analyst_count_and_age: Subqueue: count + median age in days. Today this would have shown 61→83 growth with median ~14d.
proposed_metrics.queue_history_jsonl: Append-only ledger (one line per tinker run) recording all the above metrics. Lives at monitor/tinker/queue-history.jsonl. Tinker reads tail-N entries to compute trend deltas.

data_sources: monitor/tinker/queue-history.jsonl (NEW — append-only by tinker each run, reads tail-N for trend)

tasks.description: When does tinker emit a finding vs stay quiet? Threshold logic.

proposed_thresholds.info_finding: open_issues_total grew >5% week-over-week (count this week vs count 7d ago in queue-history.jsonl).
proposed_thresholds.moderate_finding: ANY of: total > 200, OR total grew >10% WoW, OR net_velocity_7d < 0 for 2 consecutive runs, OR assigned-analyst count > 50.
proposed_thresholds.major_finding: ANY of: total > 300, OR total grew >20% WoW, OR net_velocity_7d < 0 for 4 consecutive runs, OR assigned-analyst count > 100, OR age-bucket >30d count > 50.
proposed_thresholds.operator_escalation: ANY of: total > 400, OR negative velocity for 7 consecutive runs (suggests structural problem), OR assigned-analyst >150. Tinker emits a Mode 1 finding tagged 'operator-attention-required' and recommends invoking burndown mode (mode='burndown' in decider-mode.json) OR filing a tinker DIRECTIVE for structural fix.

tasks.where_finding_lands: In tinker's daily report under findings[], with category='backlog-trend' and severity per thresholds above. Surfaces in Mode 1 quick-check summary even when tinker is otherwise in Mode 3/4. So even when tinker is busy with PROPs, the backlog finding still gets emitted.
tasks.design_note: These thresholds are first-draft guesses by operator. Tinker should sanity-check against the historical curve (e.g., 'in the 60 days before 2026-05-10, what was the actual open_issues_total range?') and propose calibrated thresholds based on real distribution. Operator wants thresholds that fire on this kind of growth pattern, not noise thresholds that fire on routine fluctuation.

tasks.description: Concrete prompt edits + new files + behavior in this PROP's scope.
expected_implementation_files: monitor/prompts/tinker.md (small dispatcher addition — read queue-history.jsonl tail-N + open-issues.json + closed-issues.json at run start)
monitor/prompts/reference/tinker-pipeline-health.md Step 4 (extend with queue-level metrics + thresholds + findings rules)
monitor/tinker/queue-history.jsonl (NEW append-only ledger — tinker is sole writer, one line per run)
build.js OWNERSHIP table (add queue-history.jsonl as git-owned append-only or workspace-owned; tinker should decide based on read pattern)
monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (if queue-history is workspace-owned, add to iteration; if git-owned, ensure it's in NEVER_PUSH-like classification or sync_glob)
tasks.backfill_question: For the first 7-14 days after this lands, tinker has no historical data to compute WoW from. Should the first runs (a) emit no trend findings until 7+ history entries exist, (b) bootstrap from closure-ledger.jsonl + open-issues.json change-history-via-git-log, (c) just count current state without trend until enough history accumulates? Recommend the cleanest path.

tasks.description: Quick retroactive check: if the proposed metrics + thresholds had been in place over the past 14 days, which findings would have fired and when? This is a sanity-check for whether the design would have actually surfaced the 200-item backlog earlier than the operator noticed it.
tasks.deliverable: A 'retroactive simulation' section in the PROP showing: 'On 2026-05-03, tinker would have emitted moderate-finding (total = 215). On 2026-05-06, tinker would have emitted moderate-finding for 2nd consecutive run, escalating to recommend burndown mode engagement.' Etc. If the simulation shows findings would have fired only on 2026-05-09 (one run before operator noticed), thresholds may be too loose. Adjust.

tasks.description: Explicitly NOT in scope for this DIRECTIVE: (a) automatically engaging burndown mode (that stays operator-authorized); (b) auto-closing stale issues (M1 already does that via decider, not tinker); (c) rewriting Step 1-3 of tinker's audit (only Step 4 + Mode 1 quick-check are extended); (d) any change to how analyst or decider read issues (purely an audit-surface addition, no behavioral change to other agents). The PROP should explicitly say 'NOT IN SCOPE' for each of these to prevent scope-creep.
tasks.deliverable: Explicit 'NOT in scope' section in the PROP.

constraints: PROP-NNN authoring authority only. Tinker proposes; operator reviews; operator (or tinker) applies per PROP scope.
Respect existing PROP-009r2, PROP-014, PROP-022, PROP-025, PROP-026, PROP-027. The new PROP should fit cleanly.
Output one PROP file under monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-tinker-backlog-trend-monitoring.json or similar.
After PROP authored, tinker may set this DIRECTIVE's status='completed' with prop_id_authored='PROP-NNN' per PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 lifecycle convention.
related_directives: DIRECTIVE-20260510-001-open-issues-backlog-clearance.json (PROP-026: the burndown framework this DIRECTIVE's audit metrics will RECOMMEND engaging, but tinker doesn't auto-engage)
DIRECTIVE-20260510-002-m1-routing-matrix-with-prop025-integration.json (PROP-027: the routing matrix that handles M1 routing decisions tinker will be measuring)

DIRECTIVE-20260510-004-decider-m1-tracker-entry-creation.json (sibling implementation-completeness fix — relates because expansion-tracker would be another thing tinker should be measuring for orphan-accumulation)
operator_notes_to_tinker: Honest framing: operator noticed the 200-item backlog before tinker did. Tinker's three most recent Mode 1 quick-checks all said 'pipeline GREEN' while it was clearly red on throughput. That's the gap this DIRECTIVE closes. Don't sugar-coat this in the PROP — name the gap, design the fix.

The 'retroactive simulation' in task_4 is important. The point of this DIRECTIVE is so tinker catches the NEXT silent accumulation before the operator does. Verify your thresholds would have done so this time.

Be conservative on the operator_escalation threshold (>400 total, etc.). The goal is for tinker to flag at the moderate-finding level BEFORE the operator has to manually engage burndown — not to be a redundant alarm AFTER everything's on fire. If your simulation shows operator_escalation would have fired AFTER moderate-finding by 2-3 weeks, the design is right.

Don't fold this into a broader 'tinker self-audit refactor' DIRECTIVE — it's narrow on purpose. Backlog trend monitoring is the one missing piece. Other tinker self-improvements can land in follow-up directives.
